title: Browser AI Features: Hidden Security Risks

modules.title: The New Browser Attack Surface: AI Assistants, Copilots, and Extensions
modules.sub: Mapping how AI tools expand your browser's vulnerability footprint
modules.description: Learners survey the range of built-in AI features and extension types now shipping in major browsers and identify the novel attack vectors each one introduces. They build a mental model of the modern browser as an expanded attack surface that traditional security evaluations were not designed to cover.

modules.title: How AI Features Leak Data: Prompts, History, and Context Sent to the Cloud
modules.sub: Tracing the data flows browser AI features create behind the scenes
modules.description: Learners examine exactly what data — including prompts, browsing history, page content, and credentials — AI browser features collect and transmit to remote servers. They assess the privacy and compliance implications of these data flows for both individual users and organizational environments.

modules.title: Malicious Extensions Disguised as AI Tools: Real Cases
modules.sub: Recognizing how threat actors weaponize popular AI extension formats
modules.description: Learners analyze documented real-world cases in which malicious browser extensions impersonated legitimate AI productivity tools to steal credentials, exfiltrate data, or establish persistence. They identify the behavioral and structural red flags that distinguish a malicious extension from a trustworthy one.

modules.title: Script Injection and In-Browser Agent Exploitation
modules.sub: Understanding how attackers hijack AI agents running inside browsers
modules.description: Learners explore how in-browser AI agents with elevated permissions can be exploited through prompt injection, cross-site scripting, and malicious page content to execute unintended actions on a user's behalf. They work through exploitation scenarios to understand the conditions that make these attacks possible and how to disrupt them.

modules.title: Safe Browsing Configurations for AI-Enhanced Browsers
modules.sub: Applying concrete settings that reduce AI browser risk for users
modules.description: Learners step through recommended configuration settings across major browsers — including Chrome, Edge, and Firefox — to disable, restrict, or sandbox AI features that introduce unacceptable risk. They produce a personal safe-browsing configuration checklist they can apply immediately after the module.

modules.title: Enterprise Policy: Managing AI Browser Features at Scale
modules.sub: Designing organizational controls that govern AI browser feature use
modules.description: Learners evaluate enterprise policy mechanisms — including group policy, MDM profiles, and allowlist/blocklist strategies — for controlling which AI browser features and extensions employees can access. They draft a policy framework that balances employee productivity with the organization's security and compliance requirements.

modules.title: Evaluating Any AI Browser Feature Before You Install It
modules.sub: Using a repeatable risk-assessment framework for new AI browser tools
modules.description: Learners apply a structured evaluation rubric to assess the risk profile of any AI browser feature or extension before installation, covering permissions, data destinations, update provenance, and vendor transparency. They practice the framework on real candidate tools and leave with a reusable decision-making process for future evaluations.

synopsis: Modern browsers now ship with built-in AI assistants, summarization tools, and extension ecosystems — each one a potential channel for data exfiltration, credential theft, or remote exploitation that security teams were not given time to evaluate before employees started using them. This course exposes the real risks hiding inside familiar browser AI features and gives both individuals and IT teams the controls to manage them safely.
rationale: Browser-integrated AI features have outpaced security review cycles at most organizations, creating a poorly understood data leakage and exploitation surface that sits on every employee's machine.
learning_outcome: Learners can evaluate the risk profile of any AI browser feature or extension, apply safe configuration settings for personal and organizational use, and detect signs that a browser AI tool is behaving maliciously.
source_signals: Brave Exposed a Dangerous AI Browser Vulnerability
How To Avoid OpenClaw's Massive Security Hole #openclaw #cybersecurity #ai
